title: SPOT DNA Extraction from 142mm Durapore 0.22Вµm Filters

","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"39bp1","text":"a) 10X STE: 100mM Tris-Cl (pHГўГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚Вђ8.0), 1M NaCl, and 10mM EDTA). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4iuq1","text":"c) 10.5M NH4OAC ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":11,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"24fuf","text":"d) 100% Ethanol (200 proof, molecular grade) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a6r58","text":"e) 1X TE, pH 8.0: 10mM Tris-ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВ‚Г‚ВђCl (pH 8.0) and 1mM EDTA ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7qleq","text":"f) Phenol, pH 8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8771i","text":"g) SEVAG aka chloroform: isoamyl alcohol ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fkvg8","text":"h) Phenol: chloroform: isoamyl alcohol, pH 8 (optional)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2b308","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"72kck","text":"Day 1 Note: **Process in sets of 4 samples through step #9 and spin all together.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fkjcq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8qtrk","text":"Reference:  Fuhrman et al, 1988. Extraction from Natural Planktonic Microorganisms of DNA Suitable for Molecular Biological Studies. warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9bi79","text":"Day 1 Note: **Process in sets of 4 samples through step #9 and spin all together.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a boiling water bath; this MUST be a rolling boil. Microwave water to heat faster, then put on hot plate, cover with foil, and boil. Using a full 1-2L beaker is recommended to prevent heat loss when you add samples. **Process in sets of 4 samples through step #9 and spin all together.
Place 50ml falcon tube with 142mm filter on dry ice. Filter MUST be kept totally FROZEN at all times to crush the filter.

Using the bottom of an untouched STERILE 15ml polypropylene falcon tube as a pestle, vigorously crush the 142mm filter while in the 50ml falcon until it has been pulverized and the filter material is BELOW the 10ml line. Only handle the 15ml falcon by the top lid, and use the conical base as the pestle.
Add 9mls of 1X STE directly to the crushed filter pieces.
Vortex for 10 seconds. 00:00:10
Add 1ml of 10% SDS dropвЂђwise while swirling. It is important to MIX SDS into STE buffer.
Spin briefly in Eppendorf 5810R centrifuge at 15вЂђ25ВєC by spinning up to 4000 rpm (3220xg) and immediately stopping.
Place in rolling, boiling water bath for 2 minutes. 00:02:00

Spin at 4000 rpm (Eppendorf 5810R) for 10 minutes at 15ВєC to separate filter and debris. 00:10:00 This MUST be 15ВєC (or room temp) because at lower temperatures SDS will precipitate, and you will lose DNA along with it.
Add 3mls of 10.5M NH4OAC to an Oak Ridge tube for each filter.
Pour supernatant (only) from 50ml falcon tube into Oak Ridge tube (38ml capacity).
Use 1ml pipette to transfer any remaining supernatant to Oak Ridge tube: ~0.5вЂђ1ml.
Remove any large filter pieces using STERILE 1ml serological pipette to reach the bottom and drag the filter pieces up the sides. (or with wide bore plastic pipette tip)
Add 28mls of 200 proof (100%) EtOH (molecular grade).
Invert to mix thoroughly.
Store at вЂђ20В°C overnight. 18:00:00

Pellet DNA in Sorvall RC5вЂђB at 4ВєC using the HBвЂђ4 rotor by spinning at max speed of 13,000RPM (27,900xg) for at least 2 hours. 02:00:00 Sorvall takes ~30 minutes to cool to 4вЂђ10ВєC and gauge may only read ~11000 RPM When finished for the day, turn off, let warm to room temp, and wipe out any liquid with Kimwipe. Note: this rotor pellets the DNA at the very bottom of the tube. Do not use a fixed angle rotor or DNA will be smeared along the side.
Gently pour out supernatant and dry pellet upsideвЂђdown in fume hood for at least 2 hours. 02:00:00
Resuspend in 500ul 1X TE (pH 8.0) for 2 hours at 37ВєC. 02:00:00
Transfer to 2ml nonвЂђLoBind tube.

Add 500Вµl phenol. Shelf life is ~6 months with equilibration buffer added and stored at 4ВєC. If acidic, DNA will not be in the aqueous layer. Phenol is covered with buffer so pipette from the bottom of the container and blow out the pipette before pulling up. Check every time (once per day) that phenol is equilibrated to pH 8.0. TO TEST pH: either a) test the equilibration buffer with pH paper (range 5.5вЂђ9) or b) pipet small amount of phenol into microcentrifuge tube, add equal amount of water, mix by inversion, spin to separate layers, and test water with pH paper.
Mix 5 times by gentle inversion.
Spin 2 min at 12000 rpm (13,000xg) to separate organic and inorganic phases (Beckman, beige) 00:02:00
Remove and discard bottom (phenol) layer. LEAVE INTERFACE. INTERFACE is the line between organic and inorganic phases
Add 300Вµl phenol and 300Вµl SEVAG (CHCl3:IsoamylOH 24:1 v/v) to aqueous phase. Alternatively, add 600Вµl of preвЂђmixed phenol:chloroform: isoamyl (25:24:1). Check it is at pH=8 before use.
Mix by gentle inversion 5 times.
Remove and discard bottom layer. LEAVE INTERFACE.
Add 500Вµl SEVAG (CHCl3:IsoamylOH 24:1 v/v).
Mix by gentle inversion.
Remove TOP layer (YOUR DNA) and transfer to new 2ml nonвЂђLoBind tube. LEAVE INTERFACE BEHIND. This top layer is your DNA!!!

Add 125Вµl 10.5M NH4OAC. Mix by inversion 5вЂђ10 times.* *You want to add 0.25 volumes of 10.5M NH 4 OAc and 2.2 volumes Ethanol for a final concentration of 2.5M NH 4 OAc and 70% Ethanol. We assume that extract volume is 500Вµl. If your volume differs, adjust amounts of NH 4 OAc and ethanol accordingly. For 500ul: 25Вµl NH 4 OAc: 0.25 x 500 = 125Вµl. 1375Вµl EtoH: 2.2 (500+125) = 1375Вµl

Add 1375 Вµl iceвЂђcold 100% EtOH (200 proof, molecular grade).* *You want to add 0.25 volumes of 10.5M NH 4 OAc and 2.2 volumes Ethanol for a final concentration of 2.5M NH 4 OAc and 70% Ethanol. We assume that extract volume is 500Вµl. If your volume differs, adjust amounts of NH 4 OAc and ethanol accordingly. For 500ul: 25Вµl NH 4 OAc: 0.25 x 500 = 125Вµl. 1375Вµl EtoH: 2.2 (500+125) = 1375Вµl
Mix by inversion 5вЂђ10 times. Small bubbles may appear, this is ok.
Precipitate overnight at вЂђ20ВєC. 18:00:00
Spin at max speed (approximately 13,750RPM, 12,535xg) in Beckman Microfuge E with horizontal rotor at 4ВєC (cold room) for 30 minutes to pellet most DNA in the center/bottom of tube. 00:30:00
To pellet the rest of the DNA, spin the same tube for at least 90 minutes at 14,000 rpm (20,800xg) at 4ВєC (Eppendorf 5810R) . 01:30:00
Gently pour off supernatant.
Dry pellet for at least 2 hrs by leaving open and upsideвЂђdown. 02:00:00
Resuspend 5m and CMAX samples in 50Вµl of 1X TE and 150m, 500m, and 890m samples in 30ul 1X TE for 2 hours at 37ВєC. 02:00:00
Transfer all reвЂђsuspended extract to 1.5ml Eppendorf LoBind DNA tube .
Aliquot 10Вµl of extract to an additional 1.5ml Eppendorf LoBind DNA tube for archiving.

After reвЂђsuspension, immediately quantify using PICO Green (Invitrogen). To be within range of the picogreen standard curve (05вЂђ20ng), you may need to dilute the extracts if from an initial sample volume of 10вЂђ20L seawater: 1:30 for 5m, DCM and 1:10 or 1:15 for deeper depths.
Prepare 2ng/Вµl dilution for a working stock using no more than 1Вµl of extract.
Store dilution, extract, and archive at вЂђ80ВєC Archive DNA should NOT be in the same freezer as MAIN stocks. Use the 2ng/Вµl extract for downstream ARISA PCR, etc.
creator: Jed Fuhrman
